assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with MIXED — both additive and reductive signals are present across the test file changes
assertion_results.evidence: Report table contains 'Test Change Classification | MIXED | Both additive and reductive signals present'. Detailed analysis confirms classification with specific signals listed.

assertion_results.text: The structural summary or reductive findings identify the removed test function test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers as a reductive signal
assertion_results.evidence: Reductive Finding #1 states: 'REDUCTIVE -- Function Removed: test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers was deleted from tests/api/purl_recommend.rs.' Summary table also lists this as a reductive signal.

assertion_results.text: The structural summary or reductive findings identify the relaxed assertion in test_recommend_purls_basic — the PURL assertion changed from checking a fully qualified PURL with qualifiers to checking a versioned PURL without qualifiers
assertion_results.evidence: Reductive Finding #2 states: 'REDUCTIVE -- Assertion Relaxed: In test_recommend_purls_basic, the PURL assertion changed from matching a fully qualified PURL string to matching a shorter versioned PURL without qualifiers.' Shows specific assertion text comparison.

assertion_results.text: The new test file tests/api/purl_simplify.rs is recognized as additive (3 new test functions)
assertion_results.evidence: Additive Finding #2 states: 'ADDITIVE -- New Test File: tests/api/purl_simplify.rs was created with 3 new test functions and 12 new assertions.'

assertion_results.text: The new test function test_recommend_purls_dedup in the modified file is recognized as an additive signal
assertion_results.evidence: Additive Finding #1 states: 'ADDITIVE -- New Function: test_recommend_purls_dedup was added to tests/api/purl_recommend.rs.'

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: Output consists solely of report.md and criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md. No code files modified.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: Report concludes with 'Overall: PASS' and presents detailed findings for human review. No merge action or command appears.

assertion_results.text: The test change classification is produced by the Style/Conventions sub-agent (which spawns a test classification sub-agent for modified files) — the classification verdict is attributed to the Style/Conventions domain in the report
assertion_results.evidence: Test Change Classification appears as its own top-level section in the report, separate from the Style/Conventions section. Style/Conventions contains only Convention Upgrade, Repetitive Test Detection, and Test Documentation. The classification is not attributed to or nested under the Style/Conventions domain.

assertion_results.text: The test change classification analysis is based on comparing base-branch and PR-branch file content (function additions, removals, assertion changes) — the structural and semantic assessment references test file content, not acceptance criteria or task requirements (constraint 1.18)
assertion_results.evidence: Structural scan compares base-branch version (4 test functions, 15 assertions) vs PR-branch version (4 test functions, 14 assertions). Assertion specificity analysis references actual test file content with specific assertion strings. Semantic assessment lists behaviors under test derived from test function content, not acceptance criteria.
